note: Colorbox Inline is a tiny, config-free extension of the Colorbox module: a single JS behavior driven by data- attributes that opens in-page content in a lightbox. Cases are all `recipe` (response-graded); they probe colorbox_inline-specific markup facts and behavior documented under agent/, and never mutate site state.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Colorbox Inline module enabled, I want a link that opens a hidden div with class 'user-login' inside a lightbox. What HTML attribute do I put on the link and what is its value?
evals.expected_output: Add data-colorbox-inline to the link and set its value to a CSS selector for the source element, e.g. User Login.

evals.prompt: How do I control the width and height of the Colorbox Inline modal for a specific trigger link?
evals.expected_output: Add data-width and data-height attributes to the trigger element; they override the global Colorbox width/height for that link.

evals.prompt: I want a Colorbox modal whose content is fetched over AJAX rather than already rendered on the page. Is Colorbox Inline the right module, and if not which one?
evals.expected_output: No — Colorbox Inline only shows content already present in the page DOM. For AJAX-loaded modal content use the separate colorbox_load module.

evals.prompt: Where is the admin settings form for the Colorbox Inline module and what does it configure?
evals.expected_output: There is no configuration UI, settings, or permissions for Colorbox Inline. Enabling the module is the entire setup; behavior is controlled per-element via data- attributes. Global Colorbox styling comes from the parent Colorbox module.

evals.prompt: What module does Colorbox Inline depend on, and what has to be installed for the lightbox to actually open?
evals.expected_output: It depends on the Colorbox module (colorbox:colorbox), which in turn needs the external jQuery Colorbox JS library installed (e.g. via drush colorbox:plugin).

evals.prompt: If the element I target with data-colorbox-inline is styled display:none, will Colorbox Inline still show it, and what happens after the modal closes?
evals.expected_output: Yes. If the target is not visible, the module shows it while the modal is open and re-hides it on Colorbox onCleanup, so hidden source markup is the intended pattern.

evals.prompt: Which data- attribute groups several Colorbox Inline triggers together so the modal shows prev/next navigation between them?
evals.expected_output: The data-rel attribute — give multiple triggers the same data-rel value to group them (Colorbox rel).
